# ShelfOps Case Study Template

Use this template only for real merchant work. Do not use it to present benchmark or simulated results as customer outcomes.

## 1. Merchant Context

- merchant name
- retail category
- store count
- SKU count in scope
- pilot dates

## 2. Problem Before ShelfOps

- ordering process before pilot
- main stockout and overstock pain points
- data limitations at pilot start

## 3. Pilot Scope

- ingestion path used
- stores and categories included
- recommendation workflow used
- review cadence

## 4. Data Readiness Summary

- starting readiness state
- key blockers resolved during pilot
- remaining data limitations

## 5. Model And Recommendation Scope

- active model version
- evidence source for the model
- recommendation policy version
- any important exclusions

## 6. Outcome Metrics

Every metric in this section must include provenance.

Example table:

| Metric | Before | During pilot | Delta | Provenance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Stockout events |  |  |  | measured |
| Overstock exposure |  |  |  | measured or estimated |
| Forecast WAPE |  |  |  | measured |
| Recommendation acceptance rate |  |  |  | measured |
| Buyer time saved |  |  |  | estimated |

## 7. What Worked

- operational improvements
- workflow adoption signals
- data-quality improvements

## 8. What Did Not Work

- unresolved blockers
- false positives or weak recommendations
- data quality or integration issues

## 9. Evidence Boundaries

- benchmark evidence used in the pilot should be listed separately from merchant outcomes
- simulated replay should be labeled simulated
- estimated financial impact should not be described as measured impact

## 10. Next Step

- expand
- extend
- revise
- stop
